sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Northwest Natural Gas stock hits 52-week high at 49.66 USD
feed.summary: Northwest Natural Gas Company's stock reached a 52-week high of $49.66, marking a 25.13% increase over the past year. This performance is supported by a stable 4.03% dividend yield maintained for 56 consecutive years, demonstrating strong operational efficiencies that have attracted investors. Despite the stock potentially trading above its Fair Value, recent strategic moves like issuing $200 million in first mortgage bonds and a raised price target from Stifel to $52.00 reflect continued positive momentum and growth prospects for the utility company.

feed.title: Credicorp: Q4 Financial Results Overview
feed.summary: Credicorp Ltd. announced its fourth-quarter financial results, reporting a net profit of $468.8 million, or $5.88 per share, missing analyst expectations. However, the Peruvian financial institution's net revenue of $1.8 billion, derived from $2.18 billion in total revenue, surpassed forecasts. For the full year, Credicorp posted a total profit of $1.94 billion ($24.35 per share) and $6.55 billion in revenue, with its stock price rising 19% year-to-date and 85% over the past year.

feed.title: RELX launches £450 million share buyback programme By Investing.com
feed.summary: RELX PLC has initiated a new non-discretionary share buyback program totaling £450 million, active from February 12 to March 20, 2026. This is part of a larger £2.25 billion share repurchase plan for 2026, aimed at reducing the company's capital by holding purchased shares in treasury. UBS AG London Branch will independently manage the program within shareholder-approved parameters.

feed.title: Kinsale Capital Group (KNSL) Q4 Earnings: How Key Metrics Compare to Wall Street Estimates
feed.summary: Kinsale Capital Group (KNSL) reported Q4 2025 revenue of $483.27 million and EPS of $5.81, surpassing Wall Street estimates. The company's key financial metrics, including expense ratio and combined ratio, demonstrated a favorable comparison against analyst projections. KNSL shares have seen a positive return in the past month, outperforming the S&P 500.

feed.title: Iron Mountain stock hits 52-week high at $108.18 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Iron Mountain Inc. (IRM) stock reached a new 52-week high of $108.18, reflecting a 14.14% increase over the past year. This achievement follows a strong fourth-quarter 2025 earnings report, where the company saw a 17% year-over-year revenue increase to $1.84 billion and an EPS of $0.61, both slightly surpassing analyst expectations. The positive performance indicates strong investor confidence and successful strategic initiatives.

feed.title: DHI GROUP, INC. SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: DHI Group, Inc. has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, revealing a challenging financial year with a 10% decrease in total revenues to $127.8 million and a net loss of $13.5 million. The company attributes these results primarily to macroeconomic conditions impacting its Dice segment, though ClearanceJobs saw a 1% revenue increase. DHI Group is focusing on strategic restructuring, product innovation, and capital management, including stock repurchases, to navigate market risks and capitalize on future growth opportunities in the technology and government sectors.

feed.title: Christopher Grady Joins Northrop Grumman Board of Directors
feed.summary: Northrop Grumman (NYSE: NOC) has announced the election of Admiral Christopher Grady, former vice chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, to its board of directors. Kathy Warden, chair, CEO, and president of Northrop Grumman, stated that Grady's extensive military experience will provide invaluable insights as the company focuses on delivering advanced technology to the United States and its allies. Grady, a four-star admiral, retired in 2025 after more than 40 years of distinguished military service.

feed.title: Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) Stock Price Down 1.1% - Should You Sell?
feed.summary: Alphabet's stock (NASDAQ:GOOG) experienced a 1.1% midday dip despite beating Q4 expectations with strong revenue and EPS, and significant growth in Google Cloud. While long-term prospects are supported by AI monetization and Waymo's progress, the company faces near-term challenges including an EU antitrust probe and concerns over a multi-currency bond deal. Analysts maintain a consensus "Buy" rating with a target price of $343.90, suggesting potential upside despite recent institutional selling.

feed.title: UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) Trading 3.2% Higher - Still a Buy?
feed.summary: UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) traded up 3.2% midday Friday, reaching $293.33, despite missing revenue estimates and facing mixed analyst revisions. While Optum's expansion and some earnings forecast upgrades provide positive sentiment, concerns remain regarding medical-cost inflation, flat Medicare Advantage pricing, and regulatory risks. Analysts currently rate UNH as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $372.13.

feed.title: Amprius Technologies (NYSE:AMPX) Trading Down 1.9% - Here's Why
feed.summary: Amprius Technologies (NYSE:AMPX) shares fell 1.9% on Friday, trading at $9.9970 with a significant drop in volume. Analyst sentiment remains "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $16.57, but insiders have been selling shares. The company reports increased institutional investment despite insider sales.

feed.title: Targa Resources Shares Dip After Dividend Declaration
feed.summary: Targa Resources (TRGP) shares dipped slightly after the company declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.00 per share for Q4 2025 and announced an intended increase to $1.25 for Q1 2026, subject to board approval. The company also confirmed the closure of its $1.25 billion acquisition of Stakeholder Midstream, which is expected to add significant fee-based revenue streams and enhance its Permian Basin operations.

feed.title: Patrick Dumont confirmed as new Chairman and CEO of Las Vegas Sands, Chairman of Sands China
feed.summary: Patrick Dumont has been officially appointed as the new Chairman and CEO of Las Vegas Sands Corp (LVS), effective March 1, 2026. He will also serve as Chairman of Sands China Ltd. Dumont, currently President and Chief Operating Officer, succeeds Robert Goldstein and aims to build on the company's legacy by focusing on growth and enhancing product offerings and customer experience in Macau and Singapore.

feed.title: Fund Update: 340,316 UMB FINANCIAL (UMBF) shares added to JOHNSON INVESTMENT COUNSEL INC portfolio
feed.summary: JOHNSON INVESTMENT COUNSEL INC has increased its holdings in UMB FINANCIAL (UMBF) by adding 340,316 shares, as per a recent SEC 13F filing for the Q4 2025 reporting period. The article also details other institutional investor activity, insider trading, government contracts awarded to UMBF, analyst ratings, and price targets for the stock.

feed.title: Fund Update: 508,087 COLGATE-PALMOLIVE (CL) shares added to JOHNSON INVESTMENT COUNSEL INC portfolio
feed.summary: JOHNSON INVESTMENT COUNSEL INC has increased its holdings in COLGATE-PALMOLIVE (CL) by adding 508,087 shares, as reported in a new SEC 13F filing for the 12-31-2025 period. The article also details significant movements by other institutional investors, insider trading activity (primarily sales), and congressional trading (all sales) of CL stock. Additionally, it highlights recent analyst ratings (predominantly "Buy" or "Overweight") and various price targets for CL.

feed.title: Insider Selling: RTX (NYSE:RTX) Insider Sells 17,527 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Shane Eddy, an insider at RTX Corporation, sold 17,527 shares of the company's stock for approximately $3.49 million on February 12th. This transaction comes as RTX recently exceeded Q4 earnings and revenue estimates and provided positive FY2026 guidance. The company also declared a quarterly dividend and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price close to its current trading value.

feed.title: Parker-Hannifin (NYSE:PH) COO Sells $4,659,336.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Parker-Hannifin's COO, Andrew Ross, sold 4,664 shares of the company stock for approximately $4.66 million on February 11th, reducing his ownership by over 26%. This sale followed a strong financial quarter for Parker-Hannifin, which exceeded analyst expectations for EPS and revenue, and declared a quarterly dividend of $1.80 per share. Despite the insider sale, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for the industrial products company.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) COO Sells $3,311,350.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) COO Panagiotis Tsourapas sold a total of 50,000 shares of company stock across two transactions on February 11th and 12th, amounting to approximately $4.78 million. After these sales, his direct ownership decreased by 77.07% to 10,415 shares. The article also details Colgate-Palmolive's recent financial performance, including beating Q4 estimates, declaring a quarterly dividend, and analyst ratings.

feed.title: Sysco Issues $1.25 Billion in New Senior Notes
feed.summary: Sysco Corporation has raised approximately $1.24 billion by issuing new unsecured senior notes due in 2031 and 2036. The company intends to use these proceeds for general corporate purposes, specifically to refinance short-term debt and optimize its capital structure. An analyst maintains a Buy rating on SYY stock with a $95.00 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst Spark rates SYY as Neutral due to stable performance but high leverage.

feed.title: Aehr test systems director Posedel sells $420k in shares
feed.summary: Rhea J. Posedel, a Director at AEHR Test Systems (NASDAQ:AEHR), sold 13,998 shares of common stock for approximately $420,151 on February 13, 2026. This transaction reduces Posedel's direct ownership to 71,163 shares and indirect ownership to 456,979 shares. The sale comes as Aehr Test Systems faces a decline in revenue and a net loss, despite securing a significant production order for AI processors.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Announces Forthcoming Retirement of Chief Legal Officer
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs announced that Kathryn H. Ruemmler, its Chief Legal Officer and General Counsel, will retire on June 30, 2026. This planned departure initiates a multi-year leadership transition aimed at ensuring continuity in legal strategy and regulatory oversight for the global financial firm. The lengthy lead time allows Goldman Sachs to manage succession planning effectively and minimize disruption to its legal and compliance operations.

feed.title: Humana Buys Clinic Operator MaxHealth From Arsenal Capital
authors: Michelle F Davis and John Tozzi
feed.summary: Humana Inc. has acquired MaxHealth, a Florida-based operator of health-care clinics, integrating it into the insurer’s CenterWell Senior Primary Care division. The financial terms of the deal were not disclosed in the official statement, but Bloomberg News had previously reported that Humana was close to purchasing MaxHealth from Arsenal Capital Partners for approximately $1 billion.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials raises quarterly dividend by 6.1% to $0.52/share
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials (VMC) has announced a 6.1% increase in its quarterly cash dividend, raising it from $0.49 to $0.52 per share. This new dividend will be payable on March 11, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 24, 2026. The increase signifies the company's commitment to returning value to its shareholders.

feed.title: BofA CEO Brian Moynihan's pay rises to $41 million
feed.summary: Bank of America (BofA) announced a 17.1% increase in CEO Brian Moynihan's total compensation for 2025, bringing it to $41 million. Moynihan's compensation package includes elements such as $1.5 million in base salary and the remainder in equity incentives. This move aligns with similar pay raises for top executives at other major Wall Street banks like Wells Fargo, Citigroup, and JPMorgan Chase.

feed.title: Cencora Prices $3 Billion Senior Notes Offering
feed.summary: Cencora priced a $3 billion senior notes offering with maturities from 2029 to 2056 and coupons between 3.950% and 5.650%. The company intends to use the proceeds primarily to repay debt incurred from its acquisition of OneOncology, thereby strengthening its capital structure. An analyst has a Buy rating on COR stock with a $425.00 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates it as Neutral due to factors like balance-sheet leverage and high P/E despite solid operating performance.

feed.title: How Investors Are Reacting To Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) $375 Million Power Generation Lease Facility
feed.summary: Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) secured a $375 million lease facility from Eldridge Capital Management for new power generation assets, building on its acquisition of Moser Energy Systems. This facility is crucial for its integrated power generation platform, but adds financial complexity given its current unprofitability and weak interest coverage. Investors have diverse views on the stock's fair value, highlighting a need to balance potential upsides with financing risks.

feed.title: Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) Trading 2.5% Higher on Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) saw its shares increase by 2.5% after HSBC raised its price target to $77, maintaining a "hold" rating. The rise was supported by strong Q2 earnings, increased FY guidance, significant AI-infrastructure orders, and the unveiling of a new AI chip. However, the company faces risks from rising memory costs and higher capital expenditure impacting free cash flow, alongside recent insider stock sales.

feed.title: Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) Trading Up 4.7% on Earnings Beat
feed.summary: Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) saw its stock price rise by 4.7% after reporting stronger-than-expected Q4 earnings and revenue, driven by increased demand from hyperscalers and AI infrastructure. The company also raised its AI revenue target significantly, leading to multiple analyst upgrades and higher price targets. Despite this positive momentum, risks include potential margin pressure from rising memory costs and a premium valuation, suggesting possible short-term volatility.

feed.title: Bank of America Lifts Moynihan’s Pay 17% to $41 Million for 2025
feed.summary: Bank of America's CEO, Brian Moynihan, will receive a 17% pay increase, totaling $41 million for 2025. This compensation includes an unchanged base salary of $1.5 million and $39.5 million in equity incentive awards. The pay raise follows a year where the bank improved profits, though its stock performance lagged behind competitors.

feed.title: Why Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile Are Beating the Market in 2026
feed.summary: Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile are outperforming the S&P 500 in early 2026, primarily due to strong earnings reports rather than overall sector gains. Verizon saw a significant boost from subscriber additions, AT&T's convergence strategy is driving growth, and T-Mobile impressed with updated long-term outlooks despite some quarterly misses. All three telecom giants provided reasons for investor optimism, with Verizon's turnaround being particularly highlighted.

feed.title: ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) Director Sells $151,755.00 in Stock
feed.summary: ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) Director Paul Edward Chamberlain sold 1,500 shares of the company's stock for $151,755.00 on February 12th, reducing his ownership by 3.13%. This follows another sale of 1,500 shares in November. The article also provides details on ServiceNow's recent earnings, stock performance, analyst ratings, and institutional trading activity.

feed.title: FedEx Shares Climb 2% Following Key Trading Signal
feed.summary: FedEx (NYSE: FDX) shares climbed 2% after a "Power Inflow" signal indicated significant buying activity from both retail and institutional traders. The proprietary signal from TradePulse suggested a high probability of bullish price movement, with FDX reaching an intraday high of $378.50, up 2.38% from its signal price of $369.69. This event highlights the utility of real-time order flow analytics for identifying advantageous intraday trading opportunities.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) COO Panagiotis Tsourapas Sells 15,000 Shares
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive's COO, Panagiotis Tsourapas, sold 15,000 shares of company stock for over $1.46 million on February 12th, reducing his holdings by 59.02%. This follows another sale of 35,000 shares on February 11th. The company recently reported better-than-expected earnings and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.52 per share.

feed.title: AnaptysBio (NASDAQ:ANAB) Insider Sells $540,000.00 in Stock
feed.summary: AnaptysBio (NASDAQ:ANAB) insider Eric Loumeau sold 10,000 shares of the company's stock for $540,000 on February 11th, reducing his ownership by 43.79%. Following this transaction, Loumeau now owns 12,835 shares valued at approximately $693,090. The biotechnology company also recently announced a $100 million stock repurchase plan, indicating management believes the stock is undervalued.

feed.title: American Express (NYSE:AXP) Insider Sells $2,820,301.82 in Stock
feed.summary: American Express (NYSE:AXP) insider Howard Grosfield recently sold 8,134 shares of the company's stock for over $2.8 million, reducing his position by 46.30%. The sale occurred on February 12th at an average price of $346.73 per share. This transaction, disclosed in an SEC filing, reduced Grosfield's total holdings to 9,433 shares valued at approximately $3.27 million.

feed.title: Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) CTO Sells $378,000.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) CTO Robert Blumofe sold 3,500 shares of company stock for $378,000 at an average price of $108.00 per share on February 13th, 2026. This transaction reduced his direct ownership by 19.36% to 14,574 shares. The article also notes a prior sale in January and discusses the stock's recent performance, institutional holdings, and analyst ratings.

feed.title: Is It Time To Revisit Alnylam Pharmaceuticals (ALNY) After The Recent Share Price Pullback?
feed.summary: Alnylam Pharmaceuticals (ALNY) has seen a recent share price pullback despite strong long-term returns. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is significantly undervalued at $308, with an intrinsic value of $698.61 per share. However, its Price/Sales ratio of 12.70x sits slightly above the industry average and a calculated "Fair Ratio," indicating it might be overvalued by that metric.

feed.title: Leidos declares quarterly cash dividend of $0.43 per share
feed.summary: Leidos Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:LDOS) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.43 per share, payable on March 31, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 16, 2026. The company has a 14-year history of dividend payments and has increased its dividend for the past 7 consecutive years. Leidos, a technology and industry services provider for government and commercial customers, recently reported annual revenues of approximately $16.7 billion and is actively expanding its business through acquisitions and strategic partnerships in AI governance and federal agency operations.

feed.title: Honeywell declares quarterly dividend of $1.19 per share
feed.summary: Honeywell (NASDAQ:HON) announced a quarterly dividend of $1.19 per share, payable on March 13, 2026, to shareholders of record by February 27, 2026. This decision reflects the company's commitment to shareholder returns, marking its 41st consecutive year of dividend payments and 15th consecutive year of dividend increases. The announcement follows a strong financial performance in Q4 2025, where Honeywell exceeded analyst expectations for both earnings per share and revenue.

feed.title: Cboe Global Markets declares first-quarter 2026 dividend of $0.72
feed.summary: Cboe Global Markets, Inc. (CBOE) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.72 per share for the first quarter of 2026, payable on March 13, 2026. The company has a strong dividend history, maintaining payments for 16 consecutive years and raising them for 11 straight years. This announcement follows their solid fourth-quarter 2025 earnings which exceeded analyst expectations, though the stock saw a pre-market decline.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials raises quarterly dividend by 6% to $0.52 per share
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Company announced a 6% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $0.52 per share, payable on March 23, 2026, marking its twelfth consecutive year of dividend increases. The company, a leading producer of construction aggregates, attributes this to strong operational and financial performance, including a 6.54% revenue growth, and an impressive 55-year streak of maintaining dividend payments. This dividend hike aligns with its commitment to delivering long-term value to shareholders.

feed.title: Former Joint Chiefs vice chairman joins Northrop Grumman board
feed.summary: Northrop Grumman Corporation has elected retired Admiral Christopher Grady, former vice chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, to its board of directors. Grady's extensive military background and leadership experience are expected to provide valuable insights to the aerospace and defense company. Northrop Grumman stock has seen significant growth over the past year, trading near its 52-week high, and the company has strong financial fundamentals, maintaining dividend payments for 56 consecutive years.

feed.title: X3 Holdings seeks Nasdaq hearing after delisting notice over $1 rule
feed.summary: X3 Holdings (Nasdaq: XTKG) faces delisting from Nasdaq due to its stock closing below $1 for 32 consecutive business days. The company's recent one-for-six reverse stock split made it ineligible for the standard 180-day compliance period. XTKG has requested a hearing before the Nasdaq Hearings Panel to appeal the decision and temporarily stay the suspension, though there's no guarantee of success.

feed.title: X3 Holdings to consolidate shares; small investors paid for fractions
feed.summary: X3 Holdings (Nasdaq: XTKG) announced that its shareholders approved a share consolidation plan at its Annual General Meeting on February 9, 2026. This measure is intended to help the company regain compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price rule. Fractional shares resulting from the consolidation will be redeemed for cash at the post-consolidation closing price.

feed.title: EIX Downgraded by UBS with New Price Target of $78 | EIX Stock N
feed.summary: UBS has downgraded Edison International (EIX) from a "Buy" to a "Neutral" rating, although it concurrently increased the price target from $70.00 to $78.00. This adjustment reflects an improved valuation outlook despite the rating downgrade. The article also provides a summary of recent analyst ratings and price targets from other firms, alongside the average Wall Street analyst forecast and GuruFocus' estimated fair value.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company (WFC) Launches New Medium-Term Note Programs
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company (WFC) has launched new Medium-Term Note Programs, Series AA and Series B, to enhance its financial flexibility and funding capabilities. This strategic move, reported on February 13, 2026, involves filings with the SEC for Distribution Agreements and global master notes. The initiative aims to optimize Wells Fargo's capital structure and support its growth strategies in an evolving financial landscape.

feed.title: SAP.DE SAP SE (XETRA) €171.72 13 Feb 2026: AI cloud momentum may raise valuation
feed.summary: SAP.DE stock closed at €171.72 on XETRA, with a gain of 1.19% on February 13, 2026, driven by enterprise AI adoption in SAP's cloud stack and a return to large-cap tech. Despite a current P/E of 27.52, the article suggests potential for re-rating if AI-led cloud revenue accelerates. Meyka AI rates SAP.DE as a "BUY" with a score of 74.26/B+ and forecasts a yearly target of €286.02, indicating a 66.50% upside, although short-term forecasts suggest possible pullbacks.

feed.title: BlackRock Canada Closes the Market
feed.summary: The BlackRock Canada team, alongside members of their Families & Allies at BlackRock (FAB) Network, participated in closing the market at the Toronto Stock Exchange to celebrate Family Day. The event highlighted learning, financial inclusion, and introduced family members to Canada's financial markets. BlackRock is a global asset manager focused on helping individuals achieve financial well-being through investment, advisory, and risk management solutions.

feed.title: Align Technology Inc. Is Quietly Printing Money – But Is This Invisible Braces King Still Worth Yo
feed.summary: Align Technology, known for its Invisalign clear aligners, is a multibillion-dollar company often in the spotlight due to its volatile stock performance. The article explores whether the company, which merges health, beauty, and tech, is still a worthwhile investment given its premium product, advanced digital dentistry technology, and significant competition. While the product remains a "game-changer" for consumers, the stock is considered high-risk with potential for good returns on dips for investors who can tolerate volatility.

feed.title: Pool Corporation Announces Director Carlos Sabater’s Planned Exit
feed.summary: Pool Corporation announced that director Carlos A. Sabater will not seek re-election after his term concludes at the company's 2026 annual meeting of shareholders. His departure is attributed to an orderly governance transition and is not due to any disagreements over company operations or policies. Analysts currently rate POOL stock as a "Hold" with a price target of $275.00, and TipRanks' AI Analyst Spark views POOL as "Neutral" due to financial stability improvements and revenue growth balanced by margin compression and a long-term downtrend.

feed.title: Most CFOs expect larger IT budgets, ‘collapsing’ staff growth: Gartner
feed.summary: A Gartner survey reveals that three-quarters of global CFOs anticipate increased technology budgets this year, with nearly half expecting rises of 10% or more, driven by digital transformation, AI adoption, and cybersecurity needs. Conversely, staff growth expectations are significantly shrinking from 6% in 2025 to 2% in 2026, indicating a shift towards optimization through automation and AI rather than headcount expansion. While overall IT spending is projected to reach $6.15 trillion in 2026, AI investment within finance tech budgets remains modest for most CFOs, though a decisive acceleration in AI spending is anticipated.
